channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [RKFORGE] Ramkrishna Forgings Submits Monitoring Reports for Preferential Issue Funds
item.description:
Ramkrishna Forgings Ltd has submitted Monitoring Agency Reports from India Ratings & Research Limited for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. These reports, mandated by SEBI regulations, detail the utilization of funds raised through two preferential issues: the allotment of 34,00,000 convertible warrants and the allotment of 6,40,000 equity shares upon conversion of warrants. The monitoring agency confirmed that there was "No deviation from the objects" regarding the utilization of these funds. The reports are in compliance with Regulation 32 of SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015, and Regulation 162A (2) of SEBI (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2018. The company has also made these reports available on its website.

  • Ramkrishna Forgings submitted monitoring agency reports.
  • Reports detail fund utilization from preferential issues.
  • Covers 3.4M convertible warrants and 0.64M converted shares.
  • Monitoring agency found no deviation from stated objects.
  • Reports are for the quarter ended March 31, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RAMKRISHNA FORGINGS LTD. | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [JSWINFRA] JSW Infra Board Meeting on May 8, 2026 to Consider Results, Dividend
item.description:
JSW Infrastructure Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 8, 2026, to consider and approve the audited standalone and consolidated financial results for the quarter and year ended March 31, 2026. The board will also consider recommending a dividend on equity shares for the financial year ended March 31, 2026. The trading window, which was closed from April 1, 2026, will reopen on May 11, 2026.

  • JSW Infrastructure board to meet on May 8, 2026.
  • Meeting to approve audited financial results for FY26.
  • Board will consider recommending dividend for FY26.
  • Trading window reopens on May 11, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: JSW Infrastructure Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [OBEROIRLTY] Oberoi Realty Board to Consider FY26 Results, Dividend, Fund Raising
item.description:
Oberoi Realty Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 8, 2026, to consider and approve the audited financial results for the year ended March 31, 2026. The board will also consider declaring or recommending interim and/or final dividends for FY25-26. Additionally, the board will consider enabling resolutions for fund raising through non-convertible debentures, other securities, equity shares, or securities convertible into equity, subject to shareholder approval.

  • Oberoi Realty board meeting set for May 8, 2026.
  • FY26 audited financial results to be considered.
  • Interim/final dividend for FY25-26 on the agenda.
  • Fund raising via debentures/equity under consideration.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: OBEROI REALTY LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [RKFORGE] Ramkrishna Forgings fixes AGM, declares Re 1 dividend, approves ESOP
item.description:
Ramkrishna Forgings' Board of Directors approved audited financial results for Q4 and FY ended March 31, 2026, with an unmodified opinion from auditors. The company declared a 1st interim dividend of Re. 1/- per equity share for FY 2025-26, with May 8, 2026, fixed as the record date. The Board also approved the allotment of 1,64,413 equity shares to its Employee Welfare Trust under the ESOP Scheme 2023 at Rs. 556/- per share, increasing the paid-up capital. Additionally, the 44th Annual General Meeting was scheduled for August 29, 2026, and Bijay Kumar & Co. was appointed as the Cost Auditor for FY 2026-27.

  • Board approved audited Q4 and FY26 financial results with unmodified opinion.
  • Declared 1st interim dividend of Re. 1 per equity share for FY26.
  • Fixed May 8, 2026, as record date for interim dividend payment.
  • Allotted 1,64,413 ESOP shares to Employee Welfare Trust at Rs. 556 each.
  • 44th Annual General Meeting scheduled for August 29, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RAMKRISHNA FORGINGS LTD. | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [SASKEN] Sasken Technologies: Alwyn J. Boopaladoss Resigns as COO
item.description:
Mr. Alwyn J. Boopaladoss has resigned from his position as Chief Operating Officer of Sasken Technologies Ltd, effective May 1, 2026, due to personal reasons. His resignation letter, dated April 30, 2026, has been submitted, and he will ensure a smooth transition. The company has acknowledged his contributions and initiated the necessary formalities.

  • Alwyn J. Boopaladoss resigned as COO of Sasken Technologies.
  • Resignation is effective May 1, 2026.
  • The reason for resignation is personal.
  • Boopaladoss will ensure a smooth handover process.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sasken Technologies Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [BHARTIHEXA] Bharti Hexacom Board to meet on May 12-13 for Q4 FY26 Results
item.description:
Bharti Hexacom Ltd has announced that its Board of Directors will convene on May 12 and May 13, 2026. The primary agenda for this meeting is to consider and approve the Audited Financial Results for the fourth quarter (Q4) and the full financial year ended March 31, 2026. In compliance with insider trading regulations, the trading window for the company's securities has been closed from April 01, 2026, and will remain closed until May 15, 2026, inclusive of both dates.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 12-13, 2026.
  • To consider and approve Q4 and FY26 Audited Financial Results.
  • Trading window closed from April 1 to May 15, 2026.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Bharti Hexacom Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [REGENCY] Regency Fincorp Board Meeting on May 6, 2026
item.description:
Regency Fincorp Ltd has scheduled a Board of Directors meeting on May 6, 2026, to consider and approve the audited financial results for the quarter and financial year ended March 31, 2026. The board will also consider issuing call letters to warrant holders and explore fund-raising options through debt or hybrid instruments.

  • Board meeting scheduled for May 6, 2026.
  • Audited financial results for FY26 to be considered.
  • Call letters for warrant holders to be issued.
  • Fund raising options via debt/hybrid instruments to be considered.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Regency Fincorp Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [JKLAKSHMI] JK Lakshmi Cement Faces ₹391.84 Lakhs GST Penalty
item.description:
🔥 Trending

JK Lakshmi Cement Ltd received an order from the Joint Commissioner of State Tax, Durg Division, Chhattisgarh, demanding ₹179.19 Lakhs in tax, ₹33.46 Lakhs in interest, and a penalty of ₹179.19 Lakhs for FY 2023-24 due to disallowed Input Tax Credit. The company plans to appeal the order, stating that it will not have a material impact on its financial, operational, or other activities.

  • JK Lakshmi Cement faces a total GST demand and penalty of ₹391.84 Lakhs.
  • The demand arises from disallowed Input Tax Credit for FY 2023-24.
  • The company will file an appeal against the order.
  • JK Lakshmi Cement anticipates no material financial impact from this order.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: JK LAKSHMI CEMENT LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [DALBHARAT] Dalmia Bharat: Penalty of ₹11.82 Lakh Waived Off
item.description:
Dalmia Cement (Bharat) Limited (DCBL), a wholly-owned subsidiary of Dalmia Bharat Ltd, had a penalty of ₹11,82,298 waived off by the Office of Additional Commissioner of CGST (Appeals), Patna, Bihar. The penalty was initially imposed by the Assistant Commissioner, Central Tax and Central Excise, Gaya, Bihar, under Section 74 of the Central Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017 and Bihar Goods and Services Tax Act, 2017, pertaining to input tax credit claimed for FY 2017-18 under Reverse Charge Mechanism by Dalmia DSP Limited, which has since merged with DCBL. The order was received on April 30, 2026. There will be no financial impact on DCBL.

  • Dalmia Cement's ₹11.82 Lakh penalty waived off by CGST (Appeals), Patna.
  • Penalty related to input tax credit claimed for FY18 under Reverse Charge Mechanism.
  • The penalty was initially imposed under CGST and BGST Acts.
  • The order was received on April 30, 2026, with no financial impact on DCBL.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Dalmia Bharat Ltd | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [GENUSPOWER] Genus Power appoints Mohit Mathur as Chief Human Resources Officer
item.description:
Genus Power Infrastructures Ltd has announced the appointment of Mr. Mohit Mathur as its Chief Human Resources Officer (Senior Management Personnel), effective May 1, 2026. Mr. Mathur will report directly to the Managing Director. He brings over 25 years of strategic HR leadership experience, specializing in HR transformation, leadership development, and HR technology modernization across diverse global and multi-sector organizations. His background includes leadership roles at NSDC, Sterlite Power, Cushman & Wakefield, Schneider Electric, Bharti Teletech, Max Life, and Eureka Forbes.

  • Genus Power appoints Mr. Mohit Mathur as Chief Human Resources Officer.
  • The appointment is effective from May 01, 2026.
  • Mr. Mathur brings over 25 years of strategic HR leadership experience.
  • He will report to the company's Managing Director.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: GENUS POWER INFRASTRUCTURES LTD.-$ | Category: Management Change
